name: Nectarines - Halved or Sliced
recipeIngredient: An average of 17 pounds for a canner load of 7 quarts
An average of 11 pounds for a canner load of 9 pints
A bushel weighs 48 pounds and yields 16 to 24 quarts, averaging 2.5 pounds per quart
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose ripe, mature nectarines of ideal quality for eating fresh or cooking.
2. Wash the fruit either hot or raw pack.
3. Follow directions for peaches, excluding the hot water dip and skin removal steps.
4. Use the same processing time as specified for peaches.
Table 1: Recommended process times for nectarines (halved or sliced) in a boiling-water canner by style of pack and jar size:
Style of Pack | Jar Size | Altitude 0-1000 ft | Altitude 1001-3000 ft | Altitude 3001-6000 ft | Altitude >6000 ft
--- | --- | --- | --- | --- | ---
Hot | Pints | 20 minutes | 25 minutes | 30 minutes | 35 minutes
Hot | Quarts | 25 minutes | 30 minutes | 35 minutes | 40 minutes
Raw | Pints | 25 minutes | 30 minutes | 35 minutes | 40 minutes
Raw | Quarts | 30 minutes | 35 minutes | 40 minutes | 45 minutes
Table 2: Process times for nectarines (halved or sliced) in a dial-gauge pressure canner by style of pack and jar size:
Style of Pack | Jar Size | Altitude 0-2000 ft | Altitude 2001-4000 ft | Altitude 4001-6000 ft | Altitude >6000 ft
--- | --- | --- | --- | --- | ---
Hot and Raw | Pints or Quarts | 6 lb (PSI) | 7 lb (PSI) | 8 lb (PSI) | 9 lb (PSI)
Table 3: Process times for nectarines (halved or sliced) in a weighted-gauge pressure canner by style of pack and jar size:
Style of Pack | Jar Size | Altitude 0-1000 ft | Altitude >1000 ft
--- | --- | --- | ---
Hot and Raw | Pints or Quarts | 5 lb (PSI) | 10 lb (PSI)
